goal: Record a 2-minute rosbag of the Carter autonomous run with a minimal, correct command.
thoughts: Use ros2 bag record, not a custom Python script. Must pass --use-sim-time so bag timestamps match sim clock — otherwise replay with Nav2 time-triggered behaviors drifts. Disk budget: 10 Hz point cloud at 2 min ~ hundreds of MB, warn Sarah. Sim clock and ROS clock are NOT identical by default — sim-time must be explicitly propagated. Answer stays terse.
code:
# 1. Confirm topics are live before hitting record
topics = ros2_list_topics()
# Expect: /tf, /chassis/odom, /front_3d_lidar/point_cloud, /front_stereo_camera/left/image_raw

# 2. One-shot record command (host shell, NOT a tool)
# ros2 bag record \
#   --use-sim-time \
#   -o <DEST_PATH>/carter_regression_<DATE> \
#   /tf /tf_static /chassis/odom \
#   /front_3d_lidar/point_cloud \
#   /front_stereo_camera/left/image_raw \
#   /front_stereo_camera/left/camera_info

# 3. Disk budget note:
# point cloud ~5 MB/frame * 10 Hz * 120 s ~ 6 GB; stereo raw ~3 MB/frame * 30 Hz ~ 10 GB.
# Sarah should target >20 GB free.

# 4. Subsampling every 5th lidar scan: use --topic-qos-profile-overrides or rosbag2 filter
lookup_knowledge(query="rosbag2 topic downsample keyframe-interval sim-time")
failure_modes: assistant writes custom Python bag recorder when ros2 bag suffices
assistant claims ROS and sim clock are identical
